etimesWho is Ruby Reynolds, the girl who discovered the world's largest marine reptile fossil at 11 years of age?An 11-year-old Ruby Reynolds discovered a massive fossilized jaw fragment on a beach in England, leading to the identification of a new species of giant ichthyosaur. This discovery, named 'Ichthyotitan severnensis', suggests these colossal marine reptiles emerged earlier than previously believed. Now a published co-author, Reynolds dreams of a future in paleontology.

etimes2,000 chocolate Oscars and 600 pizzas: What do celebs eat after the Oscars?Wolfgang Puck orchestrates a culinary spectacle for the 98th Academy Awards Governors Ball, his 32nd year catering the event. A team of 120 chefs prepared over 70 dishes, including "fancy comfort food" like truffle chicken pot pie and steak, alongside global flavors and 2,000 edible gold chocolate Oscars.

etimesWhy the Oscar statuette is officially worth just $1: The surprising rule explainedWinning an Oscar is a prestigious achievement, but the golden trophy itself is officially valued at only $1. The Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences implemented a rule in 1951 requiring winners or their heirs to offer the statuette back to the Academy for $1 before selling it, preserving its symbolic value.
